data.title: Datavault AI stock up as the company announces strategic expansions
data.description:
Datavault AI (NASDAQ:DVLT) announced on Thursday strategic expansions, which include relocation of the corporate headquarters, opening of a new center for AI and quantum computing, and international expansion.

Shares were +9.11% to $2.34 during morning trading.

The company is set to relocate its corporate headquarters to Philadelphia's downtown area from Beaverton, OR.

Also, the company has opened its Center for AI and Quantum Computing Excellence at 9040 Roswell Road in Sandy Springs, Georgia.

Datavault AI said it is actively hiring AI engineers, blockchain specialists, and systems-integration experts.

The Beaverton facility will remain operational to support WiSA Engineering, Datavault AI expansion teams, and Pacific Rim sales and customer support.

Additionally, the company is opening its Quantum Computing & Digital Twin Embassy in Knightsbridge, London, to advance European collaborations in tokenized assets and AI monetization.

data.title: Konecranes reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Konecranes press release (OTCPK:KNCRF): Q3 Order intake EUR 1,148.6 million (956.2), +20.1 percent (+22.9 percent on a comparable currency basis)
  • • Sales EUR 988.7 million (1,069.9), -7.6 percent (-5.5 percent on a comparable currency basis)
  • • Comparable EBITA margin 16.7 percent (13.4) and comparable EBITA EUR 164.9 million (143.1);
  • Operating profit EUR 153.6 million (138.1), 15.5 percent of sales (12.9)
  • Earnings per share (diluted) EUR 1.31 (1.27)
  • Free cash flow EUR 214.2 million (187.1).
  • FINANCIAL GUIDANCE
  • Konecranes expects net sales to remain approximately on the same level in 2025 compared to 2024. Konecranes expects the full-year 2025 comparable EBITA margin to remain approximately on the same level or to improve from 2024.

data.title: Gruma, S.A.B. de C.V. reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Gruma, S.A.B. de C.V. press release (OTCPK:GPAGF): Q3 revenue of $1.6B.
  • Majority net income increased 3% to $132.6 million.

data.title: Elkem ASA reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Elkem ASA press release (OTCPK:ELKEF): Q3 EBITDA of NOK 829 million compared to NOK 1241 million in the corresponding quarter last year.
  • Elkem's total operating income for the third quarter 2025 was NOK 7523 million, which was 7 percent lower than the third quarter 2024.
  • Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) was NOK 829 million, down 33 per cent from the corresponding quarter last year.
  • Earnings per share was NOK 0.05 in the quarter and NOK -0.77 year to date. EPS was negatively impacted by the results in Silicones.
  • This division has been classified as discontinued operations and assets held for sale due to the strategic review initiated to streamline Elkem's business portfolio.
  • The Silicon Products division was impacted by low silicon and ferrosilicon prices in the third quarter, while the speciality segments delivered improved results.

  • Elkem has initiated a strategic review to sell the Silicones division to streamline the company and redirect capital towards accelerating growth in the Silicon Products and Carbon Solutions divisions. An exclusive sales process is currently underway with a major industrial player that has a significant presence in the global chemicals industry. 

  • The Elkem group's equity as at 30 September 2025 amounted to NOK 23 968 million, which gave a ratio of equity to total assets of 50 per cent.

  • Net interest-bearing debt was NOK 11 666 million, which gave a ratio of net interest-bearing debt to EBITDA of 3.1x.

  • Elkem had cash and cash equivalents of NOK 3 968 million as at 30 September 2025, and undrawn credit lines of around NOK 6 000 million.

data.title: The K-shaped economic divide may have limits in sights – TS Lombard’s Dario Perkins
data.description:
The much-discussed “K-shaped” economy, characterized by divergent fortunes between high and low-income Americans, is approaching unsustainable levels, according to Dario Perkins, senior economist and managing director of Global Macro at TS Lombard.

While asset markets remain frothy and luxury consumption robust, lower-income households and small businesses continue to struggle significantly, Perkins said. “Either the U.S. economy will deteriorate too far or – more likely – the situation on Main Street will start to improve.”

The unusual economic behavior has created confusion among investors, with GDP rising at a decent pace while employment stalls and consumer sentiment remains gloomy despite continued spending.

High-income consumers now account for “more than 85% of U.S. consumption growth since 2023 – their highest share since at least the early 1980s,” Perkins said.

This is creating a stark division between economic experiences. Meanwhile, more than half of Russell 2000 (IWM) companies are operating at a loss, “which is another historical record (particularly in non-recessionary periods),” Perkins said.

Five key factors have contributed to these widening divergences. These include a cooling labor market that has disproportionately affected low-wage workers, high borrowing costs impacting small businesses more severely than large corporations, concentrated wealth effects from the stock market, regressive policy impacts, and the AI capital expenditure boom.

“Tariffs are notoriously regressive,” Perkins pointed out, adding that combined with recent fiscal policies, these have caused “the bottom 10% of U.S. households to suffer a 7% decline in incomes versus a 1.5% increase in incomes for the top 10%.”

The growing belief that mega-companies are recession-proof is also challenged, potentially suffering from recency bias based on the COVID-19 period, he added.

Mega-tech earnings, heavily reliant on advertising revenues and discretionary consumption, are not immune to economic cycles. “Every U.S. recession in history has reduced corporate earnings, typically by around 10–30%,” Perkins said, and questioned whether tech giants can maintain their AI investments if other revenue sources disappoint.

Looking forward, he presented three scenarios for 2026-2027: continued K-shaped divergence, a recession affecting even top performers, or an economic rebound that improves Main Street conditions.

Perkins believes the rebound is most likely, with “fiscal and monetary policy both turning more reflationary” potentially unleashing significant pent-up demand.

He concluded that the current situation is “delicately poised, probably isn’t sustainable for much longer,” suggesting investors should prepare for economic conditions to change regardless of recent trends.

data.title: BHP plays down iron ore dispute with China, sees contract talks as business as usual
data.description:
BHP (NYSE:BHP) Chair and CEO Chair Ross McEwan dismissed concerns Thursday about protracted iron ore contract negotiations with China, painting discussions as business as usual and highlighting recent production results in which the miner said sales were broadly in line with the previous year.

"We’re in a commercial negotiation," McEwan told reporters following the company's annual general meeting in Melbourne. "That's it. We do it every year," adding that some previous negotiations with China have taken as long as six months.

BHP (NYSE:BHP) continues to sell the iron ore it mines and has a good relationship with customers in China, CEO Mike Henry said, adding that he does not foresee broader tensions between the U.S. and China affecting contract negotiations.

"You've got two very, very powerful nations just flexing their muscles," McEwan said. "It's not the first time this has happened."

Analysts say China appears to be attempting to take control over pricing of iron ore from the industry's giant miners, and China's establishment of a state-run iron ore buyer to coordinate purchases for most of China's steel mills "has changed the competitive dynamic," RBC Capital analyst Kaan Peker said in a recent note to clients.

Peker also questioned whether BHP (BHP) should consider some kind of alliance with rival Rio Tinto (RIO) to help the miners protect profits from their lucrative mining operations.

McEwan said there are no such discussions underway, although he and Henry added that the company will always consider the best way of using infrastructure, even that owned by others, to improve its operations.

In addressing how much of BHP’s (BHP) ore is currently sold using yuan, McEwan said the company makes less than 10% of its sales in a currency other than the U.S. dollar.

data.title: Core Scientific raised to Buy at Roth as CoreWeave deal unlikely
data.description:
Roth Capital Partners upgraded bitcoin (BTC-USD) miner and data center operator Core Scientific (NASDAQ:CORZ) shares to Buy from Neutral on the view that the odds of a merger with AI infrastructure provider CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) are getting slimmer.

CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) is trying to buy Core Scientific (NASDAQ:CORZ) to lock in power and infrastructure assets for its AI-cloud push, though CORZ shareholders and proxy advisors have argued the pending $9B deal undervalues the company and exposes them to risk, due to a falling acquirer stock.

"With increasing pushback on the merger from larger shareholders and CRWV management stating the offer is its 'best and final' (although this could always be posturing), it now appears the probability of the deal closing is diminishing," Roth's Darren Aftahi wrote in a note.

Earlier in the week, Michael Intrator, CoreWeave's (CRWV) CEO, called upon CORZ shareholders to back the current terms of his company's proposed acquisition. The vote for the transaction is scheduled to take effect Oct. 30.

Roth's Buy rating compares with the SA Quant system rating of Hold, as well as the average SA analyst rating and the average Wall Street analyst rating, both at Buy.

CORZ shares, up ~26% so far this year, slipped 0.8% in Thursday morning trading.

data.title: Nexans S.A. reports 9M results
data.description:
• Nexans S.A. press release (OTCPK:NXPRF): High 9M 2025 Group organic growth at +5.8%, including +7.7% organic growth in Q3 2025.
  • Standard sales of €5,331 million in the first nine months of 2025, up +5.8% organically year on year
  • Full-year 2025 guidance confirmed, as upgraded in July 2025 : - Adjusted EBITDA of between €810 million and €860 million
  • - Free Cash Flow of between €275 million and €375 million

data.title: TGS ASA reports Q3 results
data.description:
• TGS ASA press release (OTCQX:TGSNF): Q3 revenues of USD 388 million, a 26% sequential growth.
  • Order inflow of USD 436 million during Q3 2025 – total order backlog of USD 473 million
  • Strong cash flow reducing net debt to USD 432 million, compared to USD 479 million at the end of Q2 2025
  • Solid balance sheet allows for stable dividend payment of USD 0.155 per share to be paid in Q4 2025
  • Continued scrutiny of cash outflow - capex guidance for 2025 reduced to USD 110 million from USD 135 million

data.title: Sandisk leads gain in memory, storage names as Samsung, SK hynix likely to boost prices
data.description:
• Sandisk (NASDAQ:SNDK) led gains in memory and storage stocks on Thursday after it was reported that Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) and SK hynix(OTCPK:HXSCF) are likely to boost prices on memory.
  • The South Korean technology companies have raised their prices on dynamic random access memory and NAND flash by up to 30% for the fourth-quarter, Korean Economic Daily reported. The steep price hike is due to the surging demand for all things related to artificial intelligence, which has caused a supply shortage in both memory and storage.
  • Seagate shares rose more than 3% on Thursday, as did competitor Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC). SanDisk (NASDAQ:SNDK), which was spun off from Western Digital to focus on its flash memory business, rose more than 8.5%, while Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) tacked on nearly 1.5%.
  • Seagate, Micron, Sandisk and Western Digital did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
